title: teams — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. TenancyHooks are the tenancy module's contract for reacting to tenant-lifecycle events (provision / suspend / erase). Teams does not register a bootstrapper (nothing to hydrate at tenant-boot — teams are explicit tenant admin actions like Branches). Ships three cascade hooks: branch-close prevention, team-archive cascade, tenant-erasure purge.

hooks.name: PreventTeamOrphansOnBranchClosed
hooks.purpose: Refuse the branch close when any team referencing the branch still has status='active'. Distinct from the FK CASCADE — we deliberately BLOCK because team archive is an ops decision, not a side-effect.
hooks.returns: BRANCH_HAS_ACTIVE_TEAMS (409) with the team count in the error payload

hooks.name: CascadeMembersOnTeamArchived
hooks.purpose: Cascades: transitions every active member on the archived team to status='inactive' with left_reason='team_archived'. Fires TeamMemberLeft per member with cascade_trigger='team_archived'. Runs at priority 50 (before notification listeners) so the notification fan-out sees the inactive state.
hooks.note: Duplicated in listeners.json with the same class — hooks + listeners are two views of the same registration for policy compliance

hooks.name: PurgeTeamsForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK; writes compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity family (teams / team_members / team_trials / event_teams purged). Audit rows outlive the source rows per financial + anti-discrimination retention.
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure). Emitted metrics feed the compliance dashboard for retention proof.

no_bootstrapper.reason: Teams do not need a tenant-boot hydration path — like Branches, they're explicit admin actions. Contrast with modules like `tenancy` (bootstraps request-context) or `settings` (bootstraps config cache) that need per-request tenant hydration.
